data.title: H. pylori infections market in 6MM to grow at 9.5% CAGR during 2026-2036 with emerging therapies and strategies.
data.description: The market for H. pylori infection is undergoing changes as treatment approaches focus on tailored combination therapies. This includes triple- and quadruple-drug regimens, bismuth-based treatments, and new emerging therapies like Rifasutenizol (from TenNor Therapeutics) and Linaprazan glurate. These developments are significant amid rising cases of H. pylori infections globally. The shift towards optimized therapies is particularly crucial given the effectiveness of these new approaches in combating the infection. This marks a noteworthy trend in the market as more healthcare providers and patients turn to these advanced treatments.

data.title: Emisense introduces low-cost ePM sensors for data center & hospital generators aiming to accelerate Tier-4 compliance.
data.description: A company based in Ladera Ranch, California, revealed on March 4, 2026, that Emisense Technologies, LLC has introduced electrostatic PM (ePM) sensors tailored for major diesel generators in settings like Data Centers and Hospitals. The sensors are now on the market for operators considering acquiring new generators.

data.title: Capstone Infrastructure Corp reported Q4 & fiscal 2025 results. MD&A available. Check 'www.capstoneinfrastructure.com' & 'www.sedarplus.ca'.
data.description: Capstone Infrastructure Corporation, based in Toronto, Ontario, recently disclosed its financial outcomes for the fourth quarter and fiscal year concluding on December 31, 2025. The corporation, trading under TSX: CSE.PR.A, shared this data in their 2025 Management’s Discussion and Analysis (MD&A) alongside audited consolidated financial statements. Interested parties can access this information on the company's website, www.capstoneinfrastructure.com, and on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca. The MD&A covers essential aspects such as the "Results of Operations" and a comprehensive "Financial Position Review" specifically for the quarter concluding on December 31, 2025.

data.title: KBRA gives ratings to EFMT 2026-NQM3 with $509.6 million from 1,292 mortgages with 81.8% alternative income documentation.
data.description: A credit rating agency, KBRA, has assigned preliminary ratings to 15 classes of mortgage pass-through certificates from EFMT 2026-NQM3, a non-prime RMBS transaction worth $509.6 million. The pool consists of 1,292 residential mortgages, primarily relying on alternative income documentation such as DSCR, bank statements, and asset underwriting. 81.8% of the loans fall under this category. The majority of loans are considered non-qualified mortgages in this pool.

data.title: Automotive Properties REIT had a successful 2025, acquiring 13 properties for $200M, increased cash distributions by 2.2%, and increased rental revenue by 19.3%. Undrawn capacity under its revolving credit facilities is $102.3M and had nine unencumbered properties valued at $117M. In Q4 2025, it generated AFFO per Unit of $0.251 and had an AFFO payout ratio of approximately 82.1%. The REIT also announced an agreement to purchase a property in California for US$16.0M.
data.description: Automotive Properties Real Estate Investment Trust (TSX: APR) reported financial results for Q4 2025 and full year 2025, indicating significant growth after acquiring 13 properties for $200 million. The REIT's AFFO per Unit for Q4 2025 was $0.251, with an AFFO payout ratio of approximately 82.1%. The Debt to Gross Book Value ratio was 45.9% as of December 31, 2025, with $73.3 million in undrawn credit, $0.7 million in cash, and nine unencumbered properties valued at $117 million. In October 2025, the REIT completed acquisitions worth approximately $52.5 million and raised gross proceeds of $57.1 million through a public offering. In 2026, the REIT acquired properties in Québec and California, totaling $13.25 million and US$16 million, respectively. Financially, the REIT saw increases in rental revenue, NOI, cash NOI, and FFO. The acquisition of the Vista Property in California is expected to close in the first half of 2026, allowing the REIT to advance its growth strategy and build value for unitholders.

data.title: Surge Energy Inc. reports strong 2025 results with production exceeding estimates by 1,000 boepd, spending $10 million less than forecasted at $159.7 million, generating FCF of $119.5 million, and reducing net debt to $220.6 million. Operations involved drilling 58 gross wells in Sparky and SE Saskatchewan areas, focusing on OHML techniques at Hope Valley property, achieving increased production rates and reserves replacement ratio. Detailed financial and operating highlights for Q4 2025 and 2025 are provided. WTI crude oil prices decreased by almost US$11 per barrel.
data.description: Surge Energy Inc. has reported its financial and operating results for the quarter and year ended December 31, 2025. The company exceeded its initial 2025 production guidance with an average production of 23,491 boepd, while spending $159.7 million in capital expenditures, $10 million less than forecasted. Surge generated adjusted funds flow of $279.2 million in 2025. They reduced net operating costs to $17.91 per boe in 2025, down 11% year over year. Despite a decrease in oil prices, Surge produced free cash flow of $119.5 million in 2025, returned $86.9 million to shareholders, and reduced net debt to $220.6 million. They achieved a 136% total proved & probable reserves replacement ratio, with a TPP F&D of $14.87/boe in 2025. Surge drilled 58 gross wells in 2025, focusing on their Sparky and SE Saskatchewan core areas and achieved better-than-expected well results. In Q4/25, Surge’s production averaged 23,186 boepd, with cash flow from operating activities of $59.7 million. They paid $12.9 million in dividends to shareholders in Q4/25 and returned $0.8 million through their share buyback program. Financial highlights for the year ended December 31, 2025, include $556.35 million in oil sales, $8.04 million in NGL sales, and $7.23 million in natural gas sales. Cash flow from operating activities for the year was $265.9 million, with adjusted funds flow of $279.2 million. Production highlights show an average realized price of $66.72 per bbl for oil, $40.29 per bbl for NGL, and $1.75 per mcf for natural gas in 2025. Surge has made significant progress in reducing costs, increasing efficiencies, and delivering strong financial performance in a challenging market.

data.title: Study shows football responsible for 19% of youth sports brain injuries, followed by soccer at 11%. #sportsinjuries
data.description: Key Points: An initial investigation analyzed over 70,000 cases of traumatic brain injuries (TBIs) in youth athletic activities such as football, soccer, basketball, cheerleading, and volleyball. Football constituted the highest proportion of TBIs at 19%, followed by soccer at 11%, and basketball...
